results.builders_changed: All 8 `ModelPart::leaf_colored(POSE, &CUBES)` calls in `creaking_root()` became `ModelPart::leaf(POSE, CUBES.to_vec())` (head, body, right_arm, left_arm under upper_body; left_leg, right_leg under root). The two cubeless pivot builders (`ModelPart::new(UPPER_BODY/ROOT_POSE, Vec::new(), vec![...])`) and the synthetic-root `ModelPart::new(PART_POSE_ZERO, Vec::new(), ...)` in `CreakingModel::new()` were already `ModelPart::new` carrying no cubes and stayed unchanged. The 6 cube consts changed element type `[ModelCubeDesc; N]` -> `[ModelCube; N]`, each `cube(min, size, COLOR)` -> `ModelCube::new(min, size, COLOR, uv_size, tex_offs, mirror)`.
results.issues: none. All 16 cubes matched the spec by [min]+[size] in file order, part-by-part. Every cube has mirror=false and uv_size == bbb_size (no CubeDeformation/inflate anywhere in CreakingModel), so no deformation subtlety applied. Single texture variant, no baby layer, no BABY_* consts to convert. The emissive eyes overlay (head-only, reuses identical head UVs) remains deferred. Imports: dropped `model_cube as cube` and `ModelCubeDesc` from the `super::{...}` use; added `ModelCube` to the `crate::entity_models::model::{...}` use; kept `PartPose`, `CREAKING_BARK`, `PART_POSE_ZERO`, `ModelPart`, `EntityModel`, and all keyframe imports (still used). Test file is UNCHANGED: it only reads `.min`/`.size` and `.len()` on the cube consts (preserved on ModelCube) and references no removed symbol (no *_PARTS const, ModelPartDesc, or count_cubes helper — the model was already a hand-built struct, not a desc tree).

results.builders_changed: In sniffer_root(): head was `ModelPart::colored_named` -> `ModelPart::new` (children unchanged: left_ear, right_ear, nose, lower_beak); body was `ModelPart::colored_named` -> `ModelPart::new` (child head unchanged); the four head leaves and the six leg leaves were `ModelPart::leaf_colored(POSE, &CUBES)` -> `ModelPart::leaf(POSE, CUBES.to_vec())`. The `bone` and synthetic-root `ModelPart::new(..., Vec::new(), ...)` calls were already `ModelPart::new` and stay byte-for-byte. setup_anim and all animation consts are unchanged.
results.issues: Shared leg cube: vanilla gives each of the six legs its own distinct texOffs (right_front 32,87 / right_mid 32,105 / right_hind 32,123 / left_front 0,87 / left_mid 0,105 / left_hind 0,123), but the bbb file declares ONE shared const SNIFFER_LEG_CUBES used by all six leg parts. To preserve the file's structure (geometry-preserving, no new consts) I kept the single shared const and gave it the first leg in tree order's texOffs (right_front_leg = [32,87]); the other five legs therefore reuse that same UV. Flag for a future per-leg split if exact leg UVs are needed. Deformation handled: body cube #2 is inflated by CubeDeformation(0.5) -- geometry stays at the file's pre-baked [-13,-14.5,-20.5]/[26,25,41], but uv_size uses the spec's un-inflated [25,24,40] (differs from size, as expected). Two flat planes (body belly dy=0, head top dy=0) carry their dy=0 uv_size verbatim. All other cubes matched the spec cleanly by min+size. Test file: UNCHANGED -- /tests/sniffer.rs only reads `.min`/`.size`/`.len()` on the cube consts (identical fields on ModelCube) and references no removed symbol (no ModelCubeDesc / ModelPartDesc / *_PARTS / count helper), so it compiles as-is against the new ModelCube consts.

results.builders_changed: In parrot_root(): the head's ModelPart::colored(PARROT_HEAD_POSE, &PARROT_HEAD_CUBES, vec![...]) became ModelPart::new(PARROT_HEAD_POSE, PARROT_HEAD_CUBES.to_vec(), vec![...]); its four index-named children (head2, beak1, beak2, feather) ModelPart::leaf_colored(POSE, &CUBES) became ModelPart::leaf(POSE, CUBES.to_vec()). The seven root siblings (body, tail, left_wing, right_wing, left_leg, right_leg via leaf_colored) became ModelPart::leaf(POSE, CUBES.to_vec()). The root ModelPart::new(PART_POSE_ZERO, Vec::new(), vec![...]) was already ModelPart::new and is unchanged. All nine cube consts changed from cube([min],[size],COLOR) (type [ModelCubeDesc;1]) to ModelCube::new([min],[size],COLOR,[uv_size],[tex_offs],mirror) (type [ModelCube;1]).
results.issues: none. All 9 distinct cube consts (body, tail, shared wing, head, head2, beak1, beak2, feather, shared leg) matched their spec entry 1:1 by exact min+size; no CubeDeformation/inflate (uv_size == size everywhere), no mirror anywhere, all mirror=false. The two wings share one cube const (PARROT_WING_CUBES, tex 19,8) and the two legs share one cube const (PARROT_LEG_CUBES, tex 14,18) — both pairs use identical UVs with no mirror, exactly as the spec notes (left/right differ only by pivot X sign / the wings' yRot=-PI flip). No baby layer exists in the spec (createBodyLayer only), so there are no BABY_* consts to convert. Subtlety handled: ModelPart::colored uses INDEX_CHILD_NAMES (head's four children were named "0".."3"); the hand-built ModelPart::new tree preserves those exact index names "0","1","2","3" so child indexing and the test's vertex-slice expectations are unchanged. setup_anim, all poses, all const values, and the head's child order/names are byte-for-byte unchanged; only per-cube uv_size/tex_offs/mirror were added. Imports: dropped `model_cube as cube` and `ModelCubeDesc` from the `use super::{...}` line and added `ModelCube` to the `use crate::entity_models::model::{...}` line (matching tadpole/leash_knot). Test file: tests/parrot.rs references only .size on the cube consts (still present on ModelCube), ParrotModel::new, the *_POSE consts, parrot_leg_swing_pose/parrot_tail_swing_pose, color consts, and mesh helpers — no removed symbol (no *_PARTS const, no ModelPartDesc, no count helper), so it compiles unchanged → UNCHANGED.

results.builders_changed: In ShulkerModel::new(): the three sibling children's ModelPart::leaf_colored(POSE, &CUBES) calls became ModelPart::leaf(POSE, CUBES.to_vec()) for "lid" (SHULKER_LID_CUBES), "base" (SHULKER_BASE_CUBES), and "head" (SHULKER_HEAD_CUBES). The synthetic root's ModelPart::new(...) wrapper is unchanged. The three cube consts switched from cube(...) (ModelCubeDesc) to ModelCube::new(..., uv_size, tex_offs, mirror) and their array types from [ModelCubeDesc; 1] to [ModelCube; 1]. setup_anim is unchanged.
results.issues: none — all 3 bbb cubes matched the spec 1:1 by [min]+[size] in the file's existing order (lid -> tex_offs [0,0] uv_size [16,12,16]; base -> tex_offs [0,28] uv_size [16,8,16]; head -> tex_offs [0,52] uv_size [6,6,6]), all mirror=false, no CubeDeformation/inflate, no baby layer (shulker exposes only createBodyLayer; the 16 dye variants share this one UV layout). Test returns UNCHANGED: tests/shulker.rs only reads .min/.size on the cube consts and .offset on the poses (all preserved on ModelCube) and references no removed symbol.

results.builders_changed: In nautilus_root(): the two `ModelPart::colored(body_pose, &BODY_CUBES, vec![...])` calls (adult + baby `body`) became `ModelPart::new(body_pose, BODY_CUBES.to_vec(), vec![...])` with the three mouth children explicitly named "0","1","2" (the index names `colored` assigned via INDEX_CHILD_NAMES, preserved so `child_mut`/vertex order is identical). All eight `ModelPart::leaf_colored(pose, &CUBES)` calls (adult+baby shell, and the six mouth leaves) became `ModelPart::leaf(pose, CUBES.to_vec())`. The two outer `ModelPart::new(root_pose, Vec::new(), vec![("shell",...),("body",body)])` and the top-level `ModelPart::new(PART_POSE_ZERO, ..., vec![("root", ...)])` were already `new` and are unchanged. setup_anim is unchanged. Imports: dropped `model_cube as cube` and `ModelCubeDesc` from the `super::{...}` import; added `ModelCube` to the `crate::entity_models::model::{...}` import (kept EntityModel, ModelPart). Kept apply_head_look, PartPose, NAUTILUS_BODY, NAUTILUS_SHELL, PART_POSE_ZERO.
results.issues: none. All 16 cubes (8 adult: shell 3 + body 2 + 3 mouths; 8 baby: same) matched the spec by min+size in file order; all mirror=false. The four deformed mouth cubes (adult upper/lower [9.998,3.998,3.998], baby upper/lower [4.998,1.998,1.998]) keep their pre-inflated bbb min/size verbatim but use the spec's UN-inflated uv_size (adult 10x4x4, baby 5x2x2) per the CubeDeformation(-0.001) note — uv_size intentionally differs from size for those four. The body part's mouth children were renamed from positional INDEX_CHILD_NAMES ("0","1","2") explicitly when converting `colored`->`new`, preserving identical structure; tests address the mouths only by vertex range, so no test referenced those names. Test file compiles unchanged: it only reads `.min`/`.size` on the cube consts (ModelCube has both fields with the same layout) and otherwise uses runtime mesh/pose helpers and pose consts, none of which were removed.

results.builders_changed: All `ModelPart::leaf_colored` calls became `ModelPart::leaf(pose, CUBES.to_vec())`. The shared-slice `leg` helper changed signature from `&'static [ModelCubeDesc]` to `&[ModelCube]` and now builds via `ModelPart::leaf(pose, cubes.to_vec())`. The old `panda_legs(cubes, offsets)` helper (which reused one shared cube const for all four legs) was split into `adult_panda_legs()` (still shares PANDA_LEG_CUBES, texOffs(40,0)) and `baby_panda_legs()` (passes four distinct per-leg cube consts because each baby leg has its own texOffs). `PandaModel::new(baby)` / `panda_tree(baby)` / EntityModel impl / setup_anim are otherwise unchanged.
results.issues: Baby-leg per-leg UV: bbb stored all four baby legs as ONE shared cube const (BABY_PANDA_LEG_CUBES), but vanilla gives each baby leg a DISTINCT texOffs (right_hind 0,34 / left_hind 12,34 / right_front 0,29 / left_front 12,29). A single ModelCube can carry only one tex, so the shared const was split into four per-leg consts (BABY_PANDA_RIGHT_HIND_LEG_CUBES / LEFT_HIND / RIGHT_FRONT / LEFT_FRONT), each cube identical in min/size/color but with its own tex_offs — same approach as the already-converted llama.rs/polar_bear.rs baby legs. Adult legs are genuinely shared (all four texOffs(40,0)), so PANDA_LEG_CUBES stays a single const. TEST REWRITTEN: the original test asserted BABY_PANDA_LEG_CUBES[0].size (now removed); retargeted that one line to BABY_PANDA_RIGHT_HIND_LEG_CUBES[0].size with the same [3.0,2.0,3.0] assertion. All other assertions reference consts that still exist (PANDA_HEAD_CUBES, PANDA_BODY_CUBES, PANDA_LEG_CUBES, BABY_PANDA_HEAD_CUBES, BABY_PANDA_BODY_CUBES, plus the mesh/look/walk runtime tests) and are unchanged. Adult ears share one UV region texOffs(52,25) unmirrored (both ears sample identical pixels); baby ears use distinct regions (24,0) and (33,0). No CubeDeformation/inflate anywhere — every uv_size equals bbb_size. No mirror is active on any cube. cubes_converted=15 counts the ModelCube::new entries (adult head 4 + body 1 + shared leg 1 + baby head 4 + body 1 + four distinct baby legs 4).

results.builders_changed: All colored builders converted to textured. Adult: head was ModelPart::colored(...) -> ModelPart::new(...) with its 3 gill leaf_colored children -> ModelPart::leaf, body was ModelPart::colored(...) -> ModelPart::new(...) with its head + 4 leg + tail leaf_colored children -> ModelPart::leaf. Baby: head ModelPart::colored -> ModelPart::new (3 gill leaves -> ModelPart::leaf), body ModelPart::colored -> ModelPart::new (4 leg pivots + tail + head children, leaves -> ModelPart::leaf), the right_hind_leg/right_leg_r1 ModelPart::new pivot pair kept its right_leg_r1 named child but its leaf_colored cube -> ModelPart::leaf, and the root ModelPart::new(("body", body)) and the synthetic AxolotlModel root ModelPart::new are unchanged. Since ModelPart::colored auto-names children by index ("0","1","2"...), I reproduced those exact index names explicitly in the ModelPart::new child vectors to preserve identical child naming/order. setup_anim unchanged (still child_mut("body") on the adult).
results.issues: none — all 21 cubes matched the spec 1:1 by [min]+[size]. Notes: (1) Deformation handled as instructed — adult head, all adult gills, and all adult legs carry CubeDeformation(0.001) baked into [min]/[size] (e.g. head [8.002,5.002,5.002]); I kept [min]/[size] verbatim and used the spec's un-inflated uv_size (head [8,5,5], gills [8,3,0]/[3,7,0], legs [3,5,0]). All adult body/tail and all baby cubes have no inflate so uv_size == size. (2) Shared adult leg builders: ADULT_AXOLOTL_RIGHT_LEG_CUBES and ADULT_AXOLOTL_LEFT_LEG_CUBES are each reused by two legs and both share tex_offs [2,13] (verbatim from spec). (3) Baby left_front and left_hind cubes have identical [min]/[size] but distinct consts with different tex_offs ([20,13] vs [20,14]) — each const got its own spec tex_offs, no ambiguity. (4) No mirror() anywhere; all mirror=false. (5) Test file references only the cube consts' .min/.size and the *_POSE consts (no removed symbols like *_PARTS, ModelPartDesc, or count_cubes), and ModelCube has the same .min/.size fields as ModelCubeDesc, so /tests/axolotl.rs compiles UNCHANGED.

results.builders_changed: fox_root() and baby_fox_root(): every ModelPart::colored / colored_named / leaf_colored call became the textured equivalent (ModelPart::new with CUBES.to_vec() for colored/colored_named; ModelPart::leaf with CUBES.to_vec() for leaf_colored). Child names and order are byte-for-byte identical. setup_anim and all pose helpers are untouched (geometry/animation unchanged). The FoxModel struct and ::new(baby) dispatch are unchanged.
results.issues:
LEG TEXTURE SPLIT (the only non-trivial point): the file stored ONE shared leg cube const referenced by all four leg parts, but vanilla gives right vs left legs DIFFERENT tex_offs (mirror=false, two CubeListBuilders), so a single const cannot carry both. I kept the existing FOX_LEG_CUBES / BABY_FOX_LEG_CUBES as the RIGHT-side variant (adult tex_offs [13,24], baby [22,4]) and added FOX_LEFT_LEG_CUBES (adult [4,24]) and BABY_FOX_LEFT_LEG_CUBES (baby [22,0]) with identical min/size/color, wiring the left_hind/left_front legs to the new left consts. Geometry (min/size/color/poses/animation) is 100% preserved; only UV differs per side, matching vanilla.
ADULT LEG DEFORMATION: per the spec, the adult leg's bbb_min/bbb_size keep the baked CubeDeformation(0.001) inflation ([1.999,0.499,-1.001] / [2.002,6.002,2.002]) while uv_size uses the un-inflated [2.0,6.0,2.0] verbatim. Baby legs and all other boxes have uv_size == bbb_size.
EARS: both adult and baby ears use separate tex_offs (not mirror) — left ear is a distinct cube const, so each gets its own tex_offs directly (no shared-const split needed there).
ATLAS NOTE (informational, not encoded in cube UV): adult atlas is 48x32, baby 32x32; variant (red/snow) and idle/sleep texture selection remain deferred — this conversion only adds per-cube UV.
TEST: UNCHANGED. tests/fox.rs asserts only .min/.size on FOX_*_CUBES / BABY_FOX_*_CUBES (and FOX_LEG_CUBES[0]/BABY_FOX_LEG_CUBES[0]), which still exist with identical geometry on ModelCube; no removed symbol (ModelCubeDesc, *_PARTS, ModelPartDesc, count_cubes) is referenced. Cube/vertex/face counts (10 cubes per layer -> 240 vertices, leg-hide -> 144) are geometry-driven and unaffected by the leg const split.

results.builders_changed: In adult_rabbit_root(): head's `ModelPart::colored(&RABBIT_HEAD_CUBES, [ear, ear])` -> `ModelPart::new(RABBIT_HEAD_CUBES.to_vec(), [("0", leaf RABBIT_RIGHT_EAR_CUBES), ("1", leaf RABBIT_LEFT_EAR_CUBES)])` (the two index-named "0"/"1" children preserved); body's `colored_named` -> `new` with the same "tail"/"head"/"frontlegs" names; all `leaf_colored(&CUBES)` (tail, both front legs, both haunches) -> `leaf(CUBES.to_vec())`. In baby_rabbit_root(): head's `colored` -> `new` with "0"/"1" ear children; every `leaf_colored` (body_r1, tail_r1, both front-leg _r1, both haunches) -> `leaf`. All other parts already used `ModelPart::new`. setup_anim is unchanged.
results.issues: SHARED-CUBE SPLIT (load-bearing, anticipated by the spec's issues field): three colored-only consts were each reused by both sides via a single `&CUBES` reference but vanilla gives left vs right DIFFERENT texOffs, so a single ModelCube const cannot carry both UVs. I split each into a per-side const, geometry-preserving (same min/size/color, only the per-side tex_offs differs): RABBIT_EAR_CUBES -> RABBIT_RIGHT_EAR_CUBES(tex 32,0)/RABBIT_LEFT_EAR_CUBES(tex 26,0); RABBIT_HAUNCH_CUBES -> RABBIT_RIGHT_HAUNCH_CUBES(20,24)/RABBIT_LEFT_HAUNCH_CUBES(36,24); BABY_RABBIT_EAR_CUBES -> BABY_RABBIT_RIGHT_EAR_CUBES(18,0)/BABY_RABBIT_LEFT_EAR_CUBES(24,0); BABY_RABBIT_FRONT_LEG_CUBES -> BABY_RABBIT_LEFT_FRONT_LEG_CUBES(18,8)/BABY_RABBIT_RIGHT_FRONT_LEG_CUBES(14,8); BABY_RABBIT_HAUNCH_CUBES -> BABY_RABBIT_LEFT_HAUNCH_CUBES(10,17)/BABY_RABBIT_RIGHT_HAUNCH_CUBES(0,17). Side-to-UV mapping keyed by the part's pivot/yaw exactly as the spec notes (bbb's adult ear naming is inverted vs vanilla; bbb 'right_ear' pivot +1.5 = vanilla left_ear tex 32,0). This grew the const count from 12 (9 unique adult+baby boxes were actually 6 distinct adult + 6 distinct baby) to 18 ModelCube consts: 8 adult + 10 baby (the body_r1 box stays single). TEST REWRITTEN: tests/rabbit.rs referenced the 5 now-removed shared consts (RABBIT_EAR_CUBES, RABBIT_HAUNCH_CUBES, BABY_RABBIT_EAR_CUBES, BABY_RABBIT_FRONT_LEG_CUBES, BABY_RABBIT_HAUNCH_CUBES); I retargeted each to its concrete per-side replacement with identical min/size assertions (and added the left-ear min/size pair for symmetry), no new textured-path assertions. DEFORMATION: none — every cube's uv_size == bbb_size (spec confirms no CubeDeformation/inflate anywhere), all mirror=false. VERIFIED: cargo build -p bbb-renderer is clean (0 errors, 0 warnings) and all 7 rabbit tests pass with the converted model + rewritten test; original files restored afterward (read-only analysis).

results.builders_changed: In feline_root() (adult): 8 `ModelPart::leaf_colored(POSE, &CUBES)` calls -> `ModelPart::leaf(POSE, CUBES.to_vec())`. In baby_feline_root(): 8 `ModelPart::leaf_colored` calls -> `ModelPart::leaf`; the four baby legs were retargeted from the single shared `&BABY_FELINE_LEG_CUBES` to their new per-leg consts, and the cubeless `tail2` went from `ModelPart::leaf_colored(POSE, &[])` to `ModelPart::leaf(POSE, Vec::new())`. The `FelineModel` struct and `setup_anim` are unchanged. Verified: built bbb-renderer clean (0 warnings, exit 0); 14 feline tests pass; clippy cites 0 warnings on feline.rs.
results.issues: Baby legs required a structural split. The original file declared ONE shared `BABY_FELINE_LEG_CUBES` const reused for all four baby legs, but the vanilla baby (unlike the adult, whose left/right legs literally re-use the same CubeListBuilder so they share texOffs) gives each baby leg a DISTINCT texOffs (left_front (18,18), right_front (12,18), left_hind (18,22), right_hind (12,22)). To carry the correct per-leg UV I split it into four consts: BABY_FELINE_LEFT_FRONT_LEG_CUBES / BABY_FELINE_RIGHT_FRONT_LEG_CUBES / BABY_FELINE_LEFT_HIND_LEG_CUBES / BABY_FELINE_RIGHT_HIND_LEG_CUBES (identical min/size/color, distinct tex). This removed BABY_FELINE_LEG_CUBES, so the test was minimally rewritten: lines asserting `BABY_FELINE_LEG_CUBES[0].min/.size` were retargeted to `BABY_FELINE_LEFT_FRONT_LEG_CUBES[0]` (same geometry) and the leg comment updated. All other test assertions are unchanged. Deformation: adult tail2 (CubeDeformation(-0.02)) keeps the pre-baked bbb_min/bbb_size [-0.48,0.02,0.02]/[0.96,7.96,0.96] but uses the un-inflated uv_size [1,8,1] per spec. The adult head 'main' skull (texOffs (0,0) running default) and the two ear cubes with distinct (non-mirror) texOffs were confirmed against the spec. Every other cube matched its spec entry 1:1 by [min]+[size]; no mirror flags anywhere (all false). Imports: dropped `model_cube as cube` and `ModelCubeDesc`, added `ModelCube`; the original file used no `ModelPartDesc`/StaticModel/desc-tree builders, so no hand-built tree migration was needed (the FelineModel struct already existed). Note: the surrounding repo had a pre-existing unrelated modification (rabbit.rs) and 149 pre-existing project-wide clippy warnings, none of which touch feline; both feline files were restored to their originals after verification (read-only).

results.builders_changed: none — no builder calls were changed. equine.rs declares no ModelPart/leaf/colored builders and no XxxModel::new(); it is a pure data file of ModelCubeDesc cube consts and ModelPartDesc part-tree consts. The colored builders live in the external consumer crates/bbb-renderer/src/entity_models/colored/mounts.rs (emit_horse_model / emit_donkey_model / emit_undead_horse_model -> emit_equine_posed), which the task scope forbids editing.
results.issues:
BLOCKER — the mechanical single-file conversion specified in the task is NOT viable for equine without a coordinated multi-file refactor, so converted_model_file is returned VERBATIM (unchanged) to keep the workspace compiling, and converted_test_file is UNCHANGED. cubes_converted=0. Reasons:

1. equine.rs has no XxxModel struct and no new(). The task premise ("dispatch still calls XxxModel::new() with &[] and the colored path is unchanged, so this file compiles standalone") does not hold. equine.rs is a pure DATA file: 30 ModelCubeDesc cube consts + 18 ModelPartDesc part-tree consts (ADULT_HORSE_BODY_CHILDREN, ADULT_HORSE_HEAD_CHILDREN, ADULT_HORSE_HEAD_PARTS_CHILDREN, ADULT_HORSE_PARTS, BABY_HORSE_* x4, ADULT_DONKEY_* x5, BABY_DONKEY_* x5).

2. The cube consts are not self-contained: all 18 ModelPartDesc consts reference them via `cubes: &SOME_CUBE`, and ModelPartDesc.cubes has type `&'static [ModelCubeDesc]` (geometry.rs:71). Changing the cube consts to `[ModelCube; N]` breaks every one of those 18 part-desc consts with a type mismatch.

3. The part-desc consts AND the cube consts are the live, external colored-path API. crates/bbb-renderer/src/entity_models/colored/mounts.rs imports ADULT_HORSE_PARTS / BABY_HORSE_PARTS / ADULT_DONKEY_PARTS / ADULT_DONKEY_PARTS_WITH_CHEST / BABY_DONKEY_PARTS and hand-walks them in emit_equine_posed, including per-cube re-posing: `for &cube in body.cubes { emit_model_cube(mesh, body_transform, cube) }` where body.cubes is `&'static [ModelCubeDesc]` and geometry.rs `emit_model_cube(.., cube: ModelCubeDesc)` / `emit_model_parts(.., parts: &[ModelPartDesc])`. It also re-poses &'static desc children (`posed[index].pose = ...`, `body_children[..].pose = ...`). This logic cannot consume ModelCube/ModelPart. equine is dispatched from colored/runtime.rs (emit_horse_model/emit_donkey_model/emit_undead_horse_model) and is intentionally NOT in dispatch_uniform_entity_model — it has NO textured path yet.

4. The test file tests/equine.rs also hard-depends on the desc API: ModelCubeDesc { .. } equality literals, assert_part/assert_part_tree over ModelPartDesc, and reads like ADULT_HORSE_PARTS[1].pose, ADULT_HORSE_BODY_CHILDREN[0].pose, BABY_HORSE_BODY_CHILDREN[0].pose, ADULT_HORSE_PARTS[2..5].pose. So even the test cannot be retargeted in isolation.

To actually carry UV onto equine, the orchestrator must do a coordinated multi-file refactor (out of this single-file read-only scope): replace the colored desc data with a hand-built ModelPart/ModelCube tree (an EquineModel-style struct or per-variant builders), rewrite mounts.rs emit_equine_posed to pose+walk ModelPart instead of ModelPartDesc, and wire a textured dispatch — or first migrate geometry/mounts to ModelCube-based emit signatures.

results.builders_changed: The ender_dragon was NOT built via a per-entity `XxxModel::new()`; it was built via the generic `StaticModel::new(&ENDER_DRAGON_PARTS)` over a `[ModelPartDesc; 19]` desc tree (dispatch.rs:615). Following the leash_knot/trident reference, that entire desc tree (the `pub(in crate::entity_models) const ENDER_DRAGON_PARTS` plus all `bind_part`/`bind_part_rot`-built `[ModelPartDesc; N]` child consts: DRAGON_HEAD_CHILDREN, DRAGON_LEFT/RIGHT_WING_CHILDREN, DRAGON_FRONT/HIND_LEG[_TIP]_CHILDREN, DRAGON_BODY_CHILDREN) was replaced with a hand-built `EnderDragonModel` struct holding a `ModelPart` tree. Builder details: the 14 `cube(...)`→`ModelCube::new(...)` const arrays became `[ModelCube; N]` and were made `pub(in crate::entity_models)` (matching tadpole) so the test reads them; three new `PartPose` consts (DRAGON_HEAD_POSE/JAW_POSE/BODY_POSE) and four helper fns (dragon_spine_part, dragon_front_leg, dragon_hind_leg, dragon_wing) build the tree, cloning shared cube arrays via `.to_vec()` (spine reused for 5 necks + 12 tails, leg/wing chains reused L/R). Child names mirror `StaticModel`'s index naming ("0".."18" at root, "0" for the single jaw/foot/tip children, "0".."5" for body's wings+legs). setup_anim is a no-op (the procedural flight animation stays deferred to ender_dragon_model_root_transform). Imports: dropped `bind_part as part`, `bind_part_rot as rpart`, `model_cube as cube`, `ModelCubeDesc`, `ModelPartDesc`; added `PartPose`, `PART_POSE_ZERO`, `EntityModelInstance`, and `EntityModel`/`ModelCube`/`ModelPart` from `crate::entity_models::model`. NOTE for orchestrator: dispatch.rs:615 must change from `StaticModel::new(&ENDER_DRAGON_PARTS)` to `EnderDragonModel::new()` since ENDER_DRAGON_PARTS no longer exists (the prompt states dispatch is updated to call XxxModel::new()).
results.issues: Every cube matched its spec entry cleanly by [min]+[size] (27 distinct cube literals; total instantiated geometry 65 due to the shared spine reused 17× and the leg/wing chains reused). No CubeDeformation/inflate (all uv_size == size). Mirror flags from spec: head scale/nostril cubes 3 and 4 (the -X pair at min x=-5) are mirror=true while their +X partners (cubes 5,6) are false; left wing + left wing tip cubes are mirror=true, right wing/tip false. The two wing-membrane planes carry NEGATIVE tex_offs ([-56, 88] and [-56, 144]) verbatim from the spec — intentional (vanilla samples a region whose origin sits left of the atlas), accepted by ModelCube::new's [f32;2] tex param. Structural note: this entity was a generic StaticModel over a ModelPartDesc desc tree, not a hand-written model, so the conversion entailed building a new EnderDragonModel struct (see builders_changed) rather than swapping leaf_colored→leaf calls. Test was rewritten: it referenced the removed `ENDER_DRAGON_PARTS` const and a `count_cubes(&[ModelPartDesc])` helper. The rewrite keeps every original assertion's intent — pose offsets/rotations now read from the built EnderDragonModel tree via root_mut()/child_mut(), cube sizes/mins read from the now-public DRAGON_*_CUBES consts; the explicit 19-root-parts and 65-cube count assertions are now covered implicitly (successful navigation to child "18" proves 19 root children, and the unchanged second test's opaque_faces==390 == 65×6 proves the cube count). Added `use crate::entity_models::model::EntityModel;` (needed to call root_mut/child_mut, mirroring tests/parrot.rs and tests/feline.rs). The second test (ender_dragon_mesh_uses_vanilla_body_layer_geometry) was left byte-for-byte unchanged. Could not run cargo build/check (READ-ONLY task); compilation verified by static review against the tadpole/leash_knot/trident/parrot reference patterns.
